title: Aureline device-permission row contract
description: Boundary schema for device_permission_row_record entries frozen in docs/auth/device_permission_and_capture_boundary_contract.md. The record explains which device class is involved, what permission state applies, which actor/surface requested the permission, where processing happens, what retention/export note applies, and which revoke/system-settings/system-browser escape-hatch actions remain safe. Raw audio/video/screen bytes, raw transcripts, raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw credential material never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs and export-safe labels only.

device_permission_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this device-permission row contract. Bump only on breaking payload changes.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional $schema pointer carried on worked fixtures.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable id safe for logs, RPC, and export/support packets. Raw device identifiers, raw URLs, raw transcripts, and raw credential material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic or authoritative clock source.

export_safe_label.description: Short label safe for UI, logs, support exports, and admin exports. Raw audio, raw transcripts, raw URLs, raw device identifiers, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence safe for product copy and export. Must not embed raw URLs, raw transcripts, or raw credential material.

surface_redaction_class.description: Product-surface redaction class used by settings, auth, support, admin, and evidence records.

device_class.description: Closed device-class vocabulary for permission rows. Values are stable semantics; UI labels may localize.

permission_state_class.description: Closed permission-state vocabulary for device permissions.

actor_class.description: Actor-class vocabulary reused across auth, capture, and consent surfaces.

processing_locus_class.description: Closed processing-locus vocabulary for capture/permission disclosure. Used to communicate where processing happens before capture begins.

retention_posture_class.description: Closed retention posture vocabulary for permission/capture disclosure. This is a summary posture; detailed record governance is handled elsewhere.

action_class.description: Closed action vocabulary for safe user-facing affordances attached to permission rows.

command_id_ref.description: Optional canonical command id ref the host uses to invoke this action. When present, it must resolve to the command registry; embedded surfaces cannot mint their own command ids.

route_ref.description: Optional opaque route ref or deep-link anchor. Never a raw URL.

action_affordance.description: User-facing affordance bound to a permission row.

initiated_by_user.description: True only when the current request was user-initiated (for example via an explicit button). False for implicit extension/policy/system initiated requests.

permission_actor.description: Actor and surface attribution for a device permission request.

processing_locus_disclosure.description: Pre-capture disclosure of where processing happens.

retention_disclosure.description: Retention/export disclosure attached to a permission row.

policy_block_context.description: Optional policy context that explains policy_blocked states without exposing raw policy bundle bytes.

display_label.description: Export-safe label shown on device-permission rows (for example "Microphone access").

permission_reason_label.description: Optional export-safe reason label the row can quote without exposing raw prompts or user data.

embedded_surface_limitations.description: Optional limitations that apply when the permission request is rendered inside an embedded surface (for example "Credentials cannot be collected here").

device_permission_row_record.description: Device-permission row required by the capture boundary honesty contract.
